evidence: src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts directly reads and mutates global shot-addition context (useShotAdditionSelectionOptional + clearSelectedShotForAddition) inside a local gallery hook, creating hidden cross-module coordination.

src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts is a pass-through error facade (normalizeAndPresentError/reportRuntimeError/normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow) and is identified in the packet as a high fan-in hub, increasing coupling blast radius for error-policy changes.
src/shared/components/MediaGallery/services/downloadService.ts hard-couples transport, archive assembly, and browser DOM download triggering in one module, binding callers to browser runtime details.

UI surface is split across thin re-export layers (src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx, src/shared/components/ui/primitives/cn.ts, src/shared/components/ui/primitives/tooltip.tsx) while feature code imports mixed entrypoints, weakening ownership clarity of the UI boundary.

Media gallery responsibilities are distributed across hooks/components/services with overlapping orchestration concerns (selection state, navigation, DOM/runtime effects), e.g. useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts + ShotActions.tsx + downloadService.ts.
Project context package combines session coordination, render-debug logging, and runtime error diagnostics in one provider module (src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx), blurring responsibility.

Task modules repeatedly implement the same validate->resolve/build->createTask orchestration with bespoke parameter interfaces and manual field mapping (e.g., `imageGeneration.ts` lines 361-459, `magicEdit.ts` lines 81-223, `videoEnhance.ts` lines 112-240).

`joinClips.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` build large mutable payload objects with many conditional assignments and optional overrides (`joinClips.ts` lines 247-352, `individualTravelSegment.ts` lines 534-695), indicating missing shared payload composition abstractions.

UI orchestration hooks pass broad state/control bags across many collaborators (`useInlineEditState.ts` lines 123-247, `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` lines 14-141), increasing indirection cost and weakening interface honesty.

`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` blends DB reads/deletes, query cache patching, invalidation fan-out, and UI operation-result shaping in one hook (`lines 56-195` and `197+`), creating a tangled seam between persistence and presentation.

`ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx` composes many controller objects (`SelectionOrderController`, `NavigationController`, `SegmentController`) and passes cross-cutting concerns through a single container (`lines 48-77`, `198-260`), making boundary responsibilities hard to isolate.

`useSegmentSlotMode.ts` requires a large cross-layer prop contract (`UseSegmentSlotModeResolvedProps`, lines 26-50) that mixes navigation, persistence callbacks, rendering concerns, and state mutation hooks.

`individualTravelSegment.ts` computes identical precedence-derived values (`motionMode`, saturation, brightness) twice across neighboring builders (`lines 520-532` and `616-630`), increasing divergence risk.
`joinClips.ts` performs repetitive per-field imperative assignment for per-join overrides (`lines 324-339`), which is verbose and error-prone when fields evolve.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` has an oversized memo dependency surface (`lines 96-141`), indicating highly coupled local control flow and reduced change safety.

Rate-limit API uses mixed success/failure channels for normal control flow: `checkRateLimit` returns `operationFailure` when over-limit (with decision data embedded in `failure.cause.decision`) instead of a typed success payload with `allowed: false` (`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:122-289`).

Travel-between-images request contract is a very large partially-optional bag (`TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`), then rebuilt in `generateVideoService` with many conditionally-included coupled arrays and no final structural invariant check across pair-array lengths (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts:198-285`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:448-531`).

Public-facing function names and shapes around edge JSON handling are coherent (`parseJsonBody`, strict wrapper, unified error envelope), but style diverges from the rate-limit contract style where domain-denied is encoded as operation failure (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:151-232`, `supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:171-289`).

Several files use high-volume instructional commentary and section banners around simple declarations/helpers (e.g., `src/shared/lib/vaceDefaults.ts`, `src/shared/lib/videoUtils.ts`, `src/shared/types/imageHandlers.ts`, `src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts`).
Comments frequently restate what code already says (for example default/fallback behavior and type aliases) rather than documenting non-obvious constraints.
This verbosity pattern appears consistently across unrelated modules, suggesting generated-style template expansion rather than targeted documentation.

Deprecated compatibility shims remain active in runtime-facing modules: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/legacy/testing/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`.
Legacy fields are still part of exported task-input surfaces (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` includes legacy read types and imports legacy field definitions).

Task ID extraction still resolves transitional key variants (`src/shared/lib/taskIdHelpers.ts` includes `orchestrator_task_id` and nested compatibility paths), indicating migration closure has not been enforced at boundary layers.
`src/shared/hooks/` is a flat bucket with 74 production files (holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles), far above the >10 mixed-concern split threshold.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` is physically under hooks tests but imports `@/shared/settings/hooks/useEditVideoSettings` (line 2), showing import-affinity mismatch to settings.

`src/shared/lib/__tests__/` has 69 tests but imports from multiple non-lib packages in this batch: `@/shared/services/network/networkStatusManager` (`NetworkStatusManager.test.ts:8`) and `../../components/ui/Slot` (`Slot.test.tsx:2`), indicating a mixed-concern test bucket.

Task payload construction is split across multiple ad hoc builders with repeated precedence/default rules (e.g., `buildOrchestratorDetails`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`, and `buildIndividualSegmentParamsPayload` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`).

Orchestrator-style hooks have grown into multi-responsibility hubs that combine UI state, domain policy, persistence side effects, and transport calls (notably `useTimelineOrchestrator`, `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks`, and `useVideoEditing`).

Lightbox behavior is implemented in parallel control paths (gallery lightbox component logic, shot-image-manager lightbox hook, and desktop lightbox prefetch/navigation behavior), increasing drift risk for navigation and prefetch semantics.

Desktop/mobile shot manager behavior diverges structurally: desktop composes multiple focused hooks while mobile inlines optimistic reorder, selection, reconciliation timers, and workflow state in one component.

Multiple assigned files follow the newer canonical helper naming (`normalizeAndPresentError`, `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`) while legacy naming still exists in compatibility modules (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow`), indicating ongoing but controlled migration in naming conventions.

UI/shared modules in this batch remain structurally consistent (barrel/re-export patterns in `src/shared/components/ui/primitives/*` and `src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx` are internally consistent).
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error-helper naming migration is incomplete across active callers, leaving multiple conventions in circulation.

evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` uses `reportError`/`reportAndRethrow` while many sibling files in this batch use `normalizeAndPresentError`/`normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps deprecated wrappers active with removal target `2026-06-30`, so multiple error-entrypoint APIs are simultaneously available to call sites.
This split encourages divergent call-site behavior (different helper families, mixed migration state) rather than a single enforced runtime error contract.
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` hard-requires service-role auth (`if (!auth.isServiceRole) ... 403`).

`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` and `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` gate only on `assertCompletionAuthContext(...)`, which allows non-service callers when `taskOwnerVerified` is true.

The complete-task path performs privileged state transitions (task completion updates and downstream billing trigger orchestration), but uses a looser authorization contract than the sibling billing endpoint.

findings.summary: Gallery handlers perform implicit cross-module state handoff via global selection context.
evidence: useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts reads useShotAdditionSelectionOptional(), then in useEffect calls handleShotChange(selectedShotId) and clearSelectedShotForAddition(), coupling gallery lifecycle to external mutable context.
ShotActions.tsx independently mutates local selection/setLastAffectedShotId, creating dual pathways (local props + global context) for shot-selection flow.
findings.suggestion: Move shot-addition transfer into an explicit boundary: have parent orchestration resolve pending global selection once and pass a concrete selected-shot prop/callback to gallery hooks; remove direct context reads from useMediaGalleryHandlers.

findings.summary: Central error facade is a high-fan-in pass-through hub that amplifies coupling.
evidence: runtimeError.ts exports alias types and wrapper functions that only forward to presentationReporter with no local policy logic.
Assigned files consume shared error-reporting entrypoints broadly (reportError/reportAndRethrow/normalizeAndPresentError), concentrating policy coupling at one shared hub.
Packet holistic context flags runtimeError.ts as a god module with very high importer count, indicating large blast radius.
findings.suggestion: Reduce central hub coupling by splitting error APIs by concern (normalize-only, report-only, throw) and migrate call sites to narrower imports; keep runtimeError as an internal compatibility layer during migration, then remove pass-through aliases.

findings.summary: UI module ownership is blurred by multiple thin re-export layers and mixed import paths.
evidence: button.tsx/cn.ts/tooltip.tsx are single-line re-exports with no behavior.

Consumers import from different UI strata (theme/primitives/contracts transitively), e.g. label.tsx -> primitives/cn -> contracts/cn while feature components import primitives/tooltip and theme/button directly.
This layering pattern provides little semantic distinction, so boundary intent is unclear to callers.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical UI import surface (or one per clearly distinct layer) and enforce it via lint/path rules; remove redundant pass-through files that do not establish a meaningful boundary.

findings.summary: Media gallery subsystem mixes orchestration, platform runtime effects, and data transfer concerns.
evidence: downloadService.ts combines network transfer (XHR/fetch), archive creation (dynamic jszip import), and DOM-triggered download behavior in one service.
useMobileInteractions.ts manages global window/document listeners and tap gesture policy directly in feature hook scope.
useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts combines navigation orchestration, modal routing, and context synchronization.
findings.suggestion: Split gallery into explicit layers: interaction policy hooks, application orchestration hooks, and browser-infrastructure adapters (download/event bindings). Keep feature hooks consuming interfaces rather than owning DOM/network primitives.

findings.summary: Task creation abstractions are fragmented across families, duplicating orchestration and payload-construction patterns.
evidence: Each module defines its own parameter interfaces, validation logic, and conditional payload shaping before calling `createTask`.
`imageGeneration.ts` (361-459), `magicEdit.ts` (195-223), and `videoEnhance.ts` (233-240) follow the same lifecycle with inconsistent local helpers.
`joinClips.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` assemble large mutable records with hand-maintained optional fields, rather than a shared contract-first composer.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-family composition layer (schema + defaults + optional field policy + lineage contract) and migrate task modules to declarative payload specs instead of bespoke imperative builders.

findings.summary: Shot editor callback orchestration mixes data access, cache coherence, and UI semantics in one seam.
evidence: `useDeleteSegmentHandler` executes Supabase lookups/deletes, query cache mutations, broad invalidations, and user-facing error policy in a single callback (`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` lines 56-195).
Container/state hooks then compose additional navigation and UI modes on top of this mixed seam (`ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx` lines 198-260, `useSegmentSlotMode.ts` prop contract lines 26-50).
This makes integration behavior difficult to reason about or test in isolation because no boundary cleanly owns persistence vs UI workflow.
findings.suggestion: Split into distinct layers: repository operations, cache-sync service, and UI action adapters; keep hook-level callbacks thin and compose the three explicitly.

findings.summary: Lightbox/edit hooks rely on broad parameter/state bags that obscure ownership and reduce interface honesty.
evidence: `useInlineEditState.ts` forwards large mixed concern objects into multiple mode hooks (`lines 123-247`), with many values relayed unchanged.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` builds high-fan-in render models and very large memo dependency sets (`lines 14-141`, `178-240`).
`ImageLightbox.tsx` and layout components act as broad orchestration surfaces that aggregate unrelated concerns (variant lineage, edit modes, panel state, navigation) into one contract.
findings.suggestion: Define narrower feature-specific ports (e.g., `EditModePort`, `VariantPort`, `PanelPort`) and move mapping into dedicated adapters so each hook consumes minimal, domain-specific interfaces.

findings.summary: Repeated precedence resolution and manual field-copy logic creates local divergence risk in task payload construction.
evidence: `individualTravelSegment.ts` duplicates calculation of `motionMode`, saturation, and brightness in two nearby builders (`lines 520-532` and `616-630`).
`joinClips.ts` copies many override fields one by one inside a loop (`lines 324-339`) instead of using a reusable mapper.
`imageGeneration.ts` still mixes custom conditional object assembly with helper-based composition (`lines 398-445`), showing inconsistent local style and higher maintenance surface.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared local helpers for precedence bundles and declarative field maps, then reuse them across builders to keep a single source of truth for per-field behavior.

findings.summary: Rate-limit denial is modeled as an operation failure instead of a normal typed decision, forcing callers into mixed contract handling.
evidence: `checkRateLimit` returns `operationFailure(... errorCode: 'rate_limit_exceeded')` when denied and stores the usable decision payload in `cause.decision` (`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:171-186`).

`rateLimitFailureResponse` must reconstruct a `RateLimitResult` from failure internals (`extractDeniedResultFromFailure`) instead of directly consuming a typed return value (`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:257-289`).

Elsewhere in shared edge request utilities, envelope handling is direct and shape-stable (`edgeErrorResponse`, `parseJsonFailureResponse`), highlighting this divergence (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:121-232`).
findings.suggestion: Refactor rate-limit checks to return a single typed success payload `{ allowed, remaining, resetAt, retryAfter? }` for both allowed/denied decisions, and reserve `operationFailure` for infrastructure faults (RPC/runtime/parse failures). Keep a thin adapter for existing callers during migration.

findings.summary: Travel-between-images request API exposes coupled arrays without a final invariant guard, so invalid combinations can pass type-checking and reach task creation.
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` combines required and many optional fields in one large interface, including several arrays that must correlate by pair count (`image_urls`, `base_prompts`, `segment_frames`, `frame_overlap`, optional per-pair arrays) (`types.ts:198-285`).

`buildTravelRequestBodyV2` includes some length checks for `image_generation_ids` and `image_variant_ids` vs `image_urls`, but no final validation that all pair-scoped arrays align to `image_urls.length - 1` before submission (`generateVideoService.ts:493-531`).
The function directly forwards this object into `createTravelBetweenImagesTask(requestBody)` (`generateVideoService.ts:678`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a validated builder for the public request contract (e.g., `buildValidatedTravelRequest`) that computes pairCount once and enforces all array-length invariants before task submission, returning a structured validation error when violated.

findings.summary: The video generation service is an orchestration hotspot with too many boundary responsibilities, increasing integration fragility.
evidence: Single service module handles mutation-waiting, live DB reads, image filtering, pair override extraction, model/phase decisions, payload shaping, prompt cleanup side effect, and final task creation (`generateVideoService.ts:198-689`).

Internal mechanics are exported for tests (underscore exports), which usually indicates missing stable collaborator boundaries and high coupling to internal sequencing (`generateVideoService.ts:534-556`).
The orchestrator writes directly into a broad task-input DTO rather than delegating to narrower, independently-testable boundary adapters (`types.ts:198-285`, `generateVideoService.ts:448-531`).
findings.suggestion: Split the orchestration into explicit collaborators (snapshot reader, pair-config assembler, model-policy resolver, request validator/builder, submission executor). Test via collaborator contracts and keep only `generateVideo` as public API.

findings.summary: Task-mapping seam discards status semantics when enriching generation rows, hiding degraded states from downstream flows.
evidence: Repository defines rich statuses (`ok`, `missing_generation`, `scope_mismatch`, `invalid_tasks_shape`, `query_failed`) and queryError (`generationTaskRepository.ts:7-19`, `186-249`).

Cache preloader stores status per generation (`generationTaskCache.ts:20-56`) but `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` only returns `taskId` and `taskData`, collapsing non-OK states into null-ish results (`generationTaskCache.ts:100-117`).

Adjacent consumers in this batch (e.g., lineage path) treat scope/shape failures as first-class typed errors, showing inconsistency in cross-module failure propagation style (`useLineageChain.ts:46-120`).
findings.suggestion: Propagate mapping status through the enrichment output (e.g., `taskMappingStatus`, `taskMappingError`) and let UI/polling logic branch explicitly on degraded states instead of inferring from `null` task IDs.

findings.summary: Deprecation handling is inconsistent across legacy shims, so migration deadlines are not enforced uniformly.
evidence: `toolConstants.ts` enforces via import-time `signalPastRemovalTargetUsage(...)` side effect.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` exposes collector/enforcer helpers, but enforcement only happens if callers invoke them.
`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` actively throws past removal target for legacy usage.
`handleError.ts` is deprecated with removal metadata but has no governance-policy enforcement or usage telemetry.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all migration shims behind one deprecation enforcement pattern: explicit boundary wrapper + mandatory telemetry + consistent past-target behavior (warn/throw policy from governance config), and remove import-time side effects.

findings.summary: Canonical travel/task contracts still export legacy read-shapes, keeping old schema alive in normal type surfaces.
evidence: `types.ts` imports `LegacyStructureVideoFields` and exports `TravelBetweenImagesLegacyTaskInput` in the same module as canonical task params.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines deprecated top-level fields and structure video fields still considered parseable migration input.
`taskIdHelpers.ts` keeps support for transitional `orchestrator_task_id` paths in normal helper flow.
findings.suggestion: Split legacy read contracts into an internal adapter module not exported from canonical type barrels; accept only canonical schema at main boundaries and run one explicit normalization pass for old payloads.

findings.summary: Multiple utility/type files contain generated-style explanatory bulk that outgrows the underlying logic and obscures actual invariants.
evidence: Large banner blocks and procedural guidance dominate files that mostly declare constants/types or short helpers.
Several comments restate implementation literally (e.g., defaults flow, frame quantization basics, handler parameter naming) without adding boundary rules or failure semantics.
Pattern repeats across unrelated domains, indicating template-generated documentation style rather than intent-focused docs.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to intent-only documentation: keep migration contracts, non-obvious invariants, and external coupling notes; remove explanatory narration for self-evident code and repetitive section banners.

findings.summary: Branch-heavy duplicate predicates and field mapping logic create fragile internals and raise maintenance cost.
evidence: `isVideoAny` manually checks many equivalent paths/fields with repeated conditionals.
`extractSegmentImages` and `getGenerationId` each implement bespoke fallback-priority chains for loosely shaped objects.
`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` duplicates legacy detection logic and manually strips many legacy fields during canonical conversion.
findings.suggestion: Introduce small shared selectors/predicate tables (field path arrays + iterator helpers) so fallback priority and legacy-field detection are declared once and reused, reducing branch drift.

findings.summary: Settings hook test is stored in shared hooks test bucket instead of settings test package.
evidence: `useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` imports `@/shared/settings/hooks/useEditVideoSettings` (`line 2`) while located under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/`.
Directory profile shows `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` imports from `src/shared/settings/hooks/` despite being primarily for `src/shared/hooks/`, confirming boundary leakage.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create/confirm `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/`; (2) move `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` to `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts`; (3) normalize import to local path (`../useEditVideoSettings`) and keep aliases only for cross-package imports; (4) run `desloppify move src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts --dry-run`, then apply move; (5) validate with `rg "useEditVideoSettings" src/shared/**/__tests__` and targeted `vitest src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts`.

findings.summary: shared/lib test folder hosts tests for services and UI components, obscuring boundaries.
evidence: `NetworkStatusManager.test.ts` targets `@/shared/services/network/networkStatusManager` (`line 8`) but sits under `src/shared/lib/__tests__/`.
`Slot.test.tsx` targets `../../components/ui/Slot` (`line 2`) but is also under `src/shared/lib/__tests__/`.
Directory profile for `src/shared/lib/__tests__/` has 69 files and imports from `src/shared/services/network/`, `src/shared/components/ui/`, and other folders, indicating a mixed-concern bucket.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create/confirm colocated test roots `src/shared/services/network/__tests__/` and `src/shared/components/ui/__tests__/`; (2) move `NetworkStatusManager.test.ts` and `Slot.test.tsx` first, then leave true-lib tests such as `aspectRatios.test.ts` in lib/media tests (or move to `src/shared/lib/media/__tests__/`); (3) update relative imports to local package paths; (4) run `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run` for each move; (5) validate via `rg "shared/lib/__tests__/.*(NetworkStatusManager|Slot)" -n src` and targeted vitest for moved files.

findings.summary: Shared hook package contains domain-specific orchestration rather than boundary-focused modules.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations.ts` performs domain scope verification, persistence, optimistic cache mutation, and app event dispatch in one module (`lines 55-121`, `127-373`, `419-501`).
`useExternalApiKeys.ts` combines Supabase persistence and third-party HuggingFace verification (`line 167`) under a generic shared hook location.

`src/shared/hooks/` profile shows 74 production files with heavy coupling to infra (`src/integrations/supabase/`: 41 imports) and core libs (`src/shared/lib/`: 48), indicating a catch-all ownership boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) split `useGenerationMutations.ts` into `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` (hook orchestration) plus `src/domains/generation/repository/generationMutationsRepository.ts` (DB writes/scope checks); (2) move `useExternalApiKeys.ts` to `src/features/settings/hooks/useApiKeys.ts` (or service-specific feature package) and isolate provider verification in `src/integrations/huggingface/`; (3) keep `useFileDragTracking.ts` in shared only if reused by multiple domains, otherwise move to a tool-specific hooks package; (4) apply moves with `desloppify move ... --dry-run` before editing imports; (5) run `rg "@/shared/hooks/(useGenerationMutations|useExternalApiKeys|useFileDragTracking)" src`, then `vitest` for affected tests and `tsc --noEmit` to verify import graph integrity.

findings.summary: Canonical contract hubs are pass-through facades without encoded boundary enforcement.
evidence: `client.ts` and `runtimeError.ts` are minimal wrappers over deeper runtime modules, while `public.ts` is a direct barrel with no guardrail semantics.
README positions these files as contract surfaces and relies on CI gate commands, but no boundary rule is embedded at these entrypoints.
Given importer counts (158-243), undocumented deep-import usage can create architecture drift before CI catches it.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit boundary enforcement in repo policy: 1) define allowed import entrypoints for Supabase, error handling, and generation types in lint/dependency rules (`no-restricted-imports` or dependency-cruiser), 2) deny direct imports of deeper runtime modules from app/features unless allowlisted, 3) validate with `npm run test:arch` and a targeted `rg` audit for forbidden paths.

findings.summary: Canonical runtime error surface still exports a legacy alias without deprecation markers.
evidence: README contract matrix states deprecated aliases live in `handleError.ts` with removal target `2026-06-30`.
`handleError.ts` carries `@deprecated`/`@removalTarget` metadata, but `runtimeError.ts` exports `HandleErrorOptions` alias without equivalent deprecation signaling.
This leaves mixed governance signals on what is canonical vs legacy at the top-level contract surface.
findings.suggestion: Unify deprecation signaling: either remove `HandleErrorOptions` from `runtimeError.ts` now, or annotate it with `@deprecated` and the same removal target/owner metadata used in `handleError.ts`; then add/extend contract tests so deprecated symbols are only exposed from the designated legacy module.

findings.summary: Legacy UI shim folders remain dominant import entrypoints despite canonical contracts folder.
evidence: Both files are one-line compatibility re-exports to `src/shared/components/ui/contracts/*`.
They still have very high importer counts (`button.tsx` 154, `cn.ts` 137), inconsistent with a temporary shim role.
Coupling edges from shared components into shim directories are still high (35 into `ui/primitives`, 26 into `ui/theme`).
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) target folder is `src/shared/components/ui/contracts/` as sole public UI contract entrypoint, 2) move order: migrate imports of `ui/primitives/cn` first, then `ui/theme/button`, then mark shims hard-deprecated, 3) import update commands: `rg -l "@/shared/components/ui/primitives/cn|@/shared/components/ui/theme/button" src` and apply codemod replacements to contract paths, 4) validation commands: `npm run test:contracts && npm run test:arch && npm run quality:check`, then verify zero legacy imports with `rg "@/shared/components/ui/primitives/cn|@/shared/components/ui/theme/button" src`.

findings.summary: Task payload policy is duplicated across modules, creating high drift risk in defaults and precedence handling.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` repeats overlapping field-resolution policy in multiple builders (`buildOrchestratorDetails` at ~438+, `buildTaskParamsPayload` at ~512+, `buildIndividualSegmentParamsPayload` at ~612+), including repeated `motion_mode`/post-generation fields.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts` has parallel single vs batch assembly paths (`createImageGenerationTask` ~361+ and `createBatchImageGenerationTasks` ~469+) that partially duplicate reference/hires/task param composition decisions.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoEditing.ts` recomputes keeper-frame context constraints in two separate blocks (`maxContextFrames` ~295+ and again inside `handleGenerate` ~369+), showing policy duplication at call sites instead of a shared rule.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared task-payload policy layer per task family (resolution/defaulting/precedence/field inclusion) and have UI hooks only supply intent-level inputs; move repeated frame-window calculations into a single pure helper used by both validation and submit.

findings.summary: Several orchestration hooks combine too many concerns (UI, domain policy, persistence, transport), weakening composability and test focus.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` takes a very large integration surface (`UseTimelineOrchestratorProps` ~20-54) and returns a broad nested command/state object (~56-145, ~329-385), acting as a central coordinator for many subsystems.

`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` mixes remote deletes, query cache mutation/invalidation, operation-result protocol, and UI callback orchestration in one hook (`useDeleteSegmentHandler` ~56-194 plus add/create operations ~230+).
`useVideoEditing` handles selection geometry, validation, LoRA/resource wiring, task payload composition, task creation, and cache invalidation in one module (~72-546).
findings.suggestion: Split each orchestrator into (1) pure domain logic/hooks, (2) side-effect adapters (Supabase/query invalidation), and (3) UI wiring; keep orchestration hooks as thin composition layers with narrower IO contracts.

findings.summary: Lightbox navigation/prefetch/state ownership is fragmented across multiple modules with overlapping logic.
evidence: `MediaGalleryLightbox` has an oversized API partitioned into many prop interfaces (~16-93), then performs its own navigation and prefetch orchestration (~151-217).
`useLightbox` in shot-image-manager independently implements next/previous behavior and derived-context branching (~42-77), creating a second control path for navigation semantics.
`ShotImageManagerDesktop` adds another adjacent-item prefetch path (~135-160) and pending-open behavior (~162+), overlapping concerns already present in other lightbox layers.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single lightbox controller model (navigation, prefetch, context traversal, optimistic media enrichment) shared by gallery and shot managers; keep UI components presentational.

findings.summary: Desktop and mobile shot managers use different architectural patterns for the same workflow, increasing behavioral drift.
evidence: Desktop component consumes specialized hook bundles (`selection`, `dragAndDrop`, `lightbox`, `batchOps`, `optimistic`, `externalGens`) via props (~41-47), indicating decomposed architecture.

Mobile component inlines substantial state machines for selection bar timing, optimistic reconciliation, new-shot workflow, and move operations (~58-175, ~192-292), rather than reusing shared orchestration.
Both paths still rely on shared lightbox/selection concepts, but operational semantics are implemented separately, which raises regression risk when one side changes behavior.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared reducer-level primitives for selection, optimistic reorder reconciliation, and shot-creation flow, then compose platform-specific UI wrappers around that common core.

findings.summary: Error handling is split across legacy and canonical helper families in active call paths.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` imports `reportError`/`reportAndRethrow` (`line 3`) and uses them in catch/guard paths (`lines 52, 62, 71, 81, 100`).
`handleError.ts` still exports deprecated `handleError` and `handleAndRethrow` wrappers (`lines 15-26`) with future removal target, keeping old contracts callable.
Other active modules in this same batch use canonical runtime helpers directly (e.g. `useGenerationMutations` imports `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`/`normalizeAndPresentError`).
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one runtime error API family: migrate remaining `reportError`/legacy wrapper call sites to `normalizeAndPresent*`, then remove deprecated exports (or hard-block new imports via lint rule) before the stated removal date.

findings.summary: Sibling edge flows enforce different trust levels for privileged task/billing side effects.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` explicitly rejects non-service-role callers (`lines 325-336`).
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` proceeds after `assertCompletionAuthContext` (`line 132`) and then updates orchestrator task state and billing flow.
`createGenerationFromTask` similarly proceeds after `assertCompletionAuthContext` (`line 207`) for generation-creation flow, which is broader than service-role-only policy used by billing.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a single authorization policy for completion/billing critical paths (preferably service-role-only at edge handler boundary). If task-owner operation is required, isolate owner-safe actions from privileged side effects and require explicit policy checks per action.

findings.summary: Two startup/auth paths swallow errors instead of using the project error contract.
evidence: `initializeAppEnvironment()` uses `.catch(() => undefined)` for debug dynamic imports, dropping failure context entirely.
`hasStoredSession()` catches JSON/storage errors and returns `false` with no logging/normalization, making auth probe failures indistinguishable from absent session.
findings.suggestion: Route both paths through `normalizeAndPresentError(..., { showToast: false, context, logData })` so startup/auth failures are consistently observable; keep non-blocking behavior but preserve diagnostics.

findings.summary: `HeroCtaContent` exposes an `icon` variant that has no rendering branch.
evidence: `HeroCtaContentProps.icon` includes `'discord'` in its union type.
The JSX only renders icons for `'download'`, `'plus'`, and `'external'`, so `'discord'` currently degrades to no icon with no explicit fallback.
findings.suggestion: Either implement the `'discord'` branch (with a concrete icon component) or remove `'discord'` from the public prop union so type-level contract matches runtime behavior.

findings.summary: Gallery hooks contain verbose structural commentary that duplicates obvious code flow.
evidence: `useGalleryPageState.ts` includes long multi-paragraph headers and repeated section banners that restate implementation details.
`useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` keeps migration-history commentary in-file instead of concise intent/constraints, increasing maintenance noise.
findings.suggestion: Trim to short intent-focused comments (why/constraints only), move migration history to PR/docs, and keep hook files centered on executable logic.

findings.summary: Legacy DTO aliases and runtime legacy accessor keep dual-path migration debt active.
evidence: `GenerationRowDto` still embeds `GenerationRowLegacyAliases` for old transport keys.
`supabaseRuntime.ts` keeps `getRuntimeSupabaseClientForLegacy()` as a transitional API.
`legacySupabasePolicy.ts` indicates the compatibility track is still enforced via policy rather than removed.
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration in stages: 1) inventory active legacy imports/keys with `rg`, 2) migrate callers to canonical contracts, 3) gate in CI with import-budget target at zero, 4) remove legacy accessor/alias types once budget reaches zero.

findings.summary: `domains/imageEdit` is organized as a facade over shared UI internals, not a domain-owned boundary.
evidence: `src/domains/imageEdit/index.ts` re-exports hooks/components/contexts directly from `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/*` rather than domain-owned modules.
By contrast, `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts` exports local domain contracts, showing a clearer package boundary pattern.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `src/features/image-edit/` and move MediaLightbox-facing UI exports there; 2) keep `src/domains/imageEdit/` for pure domain contracts and mappers only; 3) add temporary compatibility re-exports, then update imports with `rg -n "@/domains/imageEdit|@/shared/components/MediaLightbox" src` and codemod replacements; 4) validate with `pnpm vitest` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`; 5) remove temporary re-exports once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Two UI components use over-optimized local state/comparators that risk stale rendering.
evidence: `ActiveLoRAsDisplay` custom memo comparator ignores props such as `className`, `onAddTriggerWord`, and callback identity, so meaningful prop changes may not trigger re-render.
`HeroCtaContent` mirrors props into local state with timer-driven transitions, increasing branch/state complexity for a simple display transformation.
findings.suggestion: Remove/relax the custom comparator (or include all render-relevant props), and simplify CTA transitions to derive directly from props with CSS transitions where possible.
